title: camissonia scapoidea subsp. macrocarpa
intro: Camissonia scapoidea subsp. macrocarpa, also known as the Great Basin evening primrose, is a perennial wildflower native to the Great Basin region in North America, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and ability to thrive in dry, sandy areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Camissonia scapoidea subsp. macrocarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, salves, or infusions, often used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds, or taken internally to support overall health and well-being.
